authoritative_sources.title: Construction eTool: Guide for Daily Inspection of Trenches and Excavations
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. federal workplace safety authority. This official eTool gives concrete competent-person inspection and immediate-removal expectations that materially affect whether site work can advance.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Site safety prerequisite and execution hold handling
used_for: boundary between operations tracking and safety authority

authoritative_sources.title: 2022 Construction General Permit
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: EPA's construction general permit is a primary federal permit baseline for inspection frequency, inspection reporting, record retention, and noncompliance handling in covered construction work.

authoritative_sources.title: Obtaining a Permit
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official building-department guide shows that permit application, plan filing, and permit objections sit with registered design professionals and the authority having jurisdiction, creating a clear refusal boundary for this lane.
used_for: refusal of permit approval or code-interpretation requests

authoritative_sources.title: Residential Building Final Inspection Quick Sheet
authoritative_sources.publisher: Seattle Department of Construction and Inspections
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This official final-inspection checklist gives concrete public examples of completion gating conditions: all construction complete, related permits passed, approved plans on site, and special inspection letters approved.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Final inspection and completion evidence review

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Manual, Section 5-1: Project Records and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Caltrans publishes an official owner-side construction manual that defines project records, daily reports, estimate and project status, and change documentation supporting construction execution traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Field record capture and exception traceability
used_for: record-of-truth expectations for rework and exception handling

authoritative_sources.title: P120 Project Cost and Schedule Management Policy Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA's official P120 policy defines lifecycle-spanning cost and schedule management practices, deliverables, and construction and close-out phase requirements for public-sector project execution.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Lifecycle-state progression and controls summary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Commissioning Documents: Process, Contents, and Acceptance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized federal and industry public guidance platform. This commissioning reference defines systems manuals, issue logs, training materials, and acceptance documentation needed for closeout and turnover completeness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Closeout package and turnover evidence handling
used_for: completion refusal when turnover evidence is missing

workflow_stages.name: Execution intake confirmation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the accepted work package has the governing work order, site, permit, trade, subcontractor, and jurisdiction context before progressing execution state.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Site-readiness and active-work validation
workflow_stages.description: Check site logs, approved plans, permit status, utility and access conditions, safety observations, and inspection posture before allowing work to advance.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception queue and rework handling
workflow_stages.description: Use project records, daily reports, change documentation, and discrepancy evidence to decide whether work must hold, return for rework, or escalate.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Completion and closeout evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Require final inspection, punch-list, turnover, systems-manual, and acceptance artifacts before completion is recorded.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Authority escalation and completion reversal
workflow_stages.description: Stop or reverse progression when safety, inspection, permit, trade, design, or policy conflicts materially change whether work can continue or close.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: Permits, approved plans, and jurisdiction instructions
Inspection records, special inspection letters, and deficiency logs
Site logs, daily reports, and safety observations
Schedule milestones, short-interval plans, and subcontractor commitments
Change orders, RFIs, and rework documentation
Photos, test reports, and installation evidence
Punch lists, systems manuals, training records, and closeout packages

allowed: Assess whether documented records support advancing, holding, reworking, or completing construction execution work.
Prepare evidence-backed execution updates, completion records, exception notes, and escalation packages.
Request missing prerequisites and traceability records before changing lifecycle state.
escalate: Permit status, inspection requirements, site safety conditions, or closeout evidence materially change the answer.
Change-order, design, subcontractor, or policy conflicts affect whether work can advance or close.
Licensed trade, superintendent, permitting, inspection, or safety ownership is needed to resolve the issue.
refuse: Permit approval, code interpretation, or regulator-facing commitments
Licensed trade signoff, engineer-of-record judgment, or design approval
Completion, closure, or acceptance without sufficient evidence

domain_failure_modes: Advancing work based on generic status labels instead of actual permit, site-readiness, inspection, and subcontractor records.
Marking work complete while final inspection, punch-list, systems-manual, or turnover evidence is still missing.
Failing to reopen or reverse lifecycle state when new safety observations or failed inspections change the answer.
Allowing unresolved change-order or scope mismatch conditions to disappear from the execution queue.
Overreaching into code interpretation, licensed trade judgment, design authority, or financial approval because the task sits in an operations queue.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific permit, inspection, and closeout differences
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Permit classes, inspection checkpoints, and completion evidence vary materially by authority having jurisdiction and project type.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of the governing jurisdiction, permit type, and authority checklist before final progression or completion decisions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific authority thresholds for change-order, rework, and closeout approval
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Public baselines define blocker handling, but tenant governance decides which execution changes require project controls, superintendent, or executive approval.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Retrieve the tenant authority matrix and keep outputs recommendation-only when approval ownership is unresolved.

notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for a validated spec pack because the remaining ambiguity is explicitly constrained to tenant or jurisdiction retrieval at runtime.
The research output constrains the lane to execution-state management with explicit completion-evidence discipline and permit, inspection, safety, and licensed-trade escalation.
